channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: I can't wait to test the world's first 8TB PCIe 5.0 SSD, and I'm sure this one won't be too expensive
item.description: TEAMGROUP introduces the world’s first 8TB PCIe 5.0 SSD, delivering speeds of 13,500 MB/s and advanced cooling for sustained peak performance.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Tech leaders' message to Biden
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Adata XPG SE940: A Game-Changing Portable SSD with USB 4 Technology
item.description: The Adata XPG SE940 is a revolutionary portable SSD that marks Adata's first foray into USB 4 technology, offering blistering speeds of up to 4GBps on read/write.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Adobe will kill its popular 20GB Photoshop & Lightroom plans very soon, so make sure you make the right move
item.description: Adobe phases out 20GB Creative Cloud plan, hiking the monthly costs by 50% to encourage switching to more expensive annual plans.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: These AI applications are aiding—not replacing—human creatives
item.description: There’s been plenty of speculation about whether generative AI could replace—or perhaps work alongside—human creativity. Indeed, many already use popular generative AI tools to create illustrations and even audio and video.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: H-1B: Silicon Valley’s favorite foreign-worker visa under attack, while Trump, Musk offer support
item.description: The schism in MAGA raised questions about the fate in the second Trump era of a visa seen in Silicon Valley as key to the technology industry's innovation and success. It comes at a time when the tech industry has gained unprecedented proximity to U.S. levers of power.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: El peor consejo de liderazgo recibido jamás
item.description: En algún momento de tu carrera has recibido malos consejos. Normalmente, son bien intencionados, pero tal vez estén anticuados o revelen una visión del mundo problemática en la persona que te dio el consejo, que sería mejor que no imitaras.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Stretch your AI dollar and accelerate AI adoption
item.description: Given their lack of scale and resources as compared to larger enterprises, it’s no wonder that small to medium businesses (SMBs) weigh every decision to adopt new, emerging technologies more prudently. But this also comes without the layers of bureaucracy that plague larger enterprises.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 6 years ago, Elon Musk offered help during wildfires. This time he blamed DEI
item.description: When a devastating wildfire hit California in November 2018, a powerful CEO went on Twitter to ask how his company could help. That CEO’s name was Elon Musk. What a difference six years can make.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Akeneo aims to transform the retail playbook with AI and data consistency
item.description: Australian retailers have spent much of the last few years buffeted by economic challenges. In 2024, squeezed by the rising cost of living, inflationary impact, and interest rates, they are now grappling with declining consumer spending and confidence.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Researchers build a bridge from C to Rust and memory safety
item.description: Memory errors such as out-of-bounds reads and writes and use-after-free bugs have plagued applications for decades, causing problems ranging from minor execution glitches to global security nightmares.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Flying taxis are on the horizon as aviation soars into a new frontier
item.description: Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation and nearby Wisk Aero, with ties to aerospace giant Boeing Co. and Google co-founder Larry Page, are at the forefront in the race to bring air taxis to market in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Larry Magid: Smart ring tracks health, fitness data with long battery life
item.description: Although no tracker is all encompassing and 100% accurate, the Ringconn seems to give pretty good results
item.dc:creator: Larry Magid, The Mercury News

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: Companies across the globe, at least 4 in 10, are likely to cut jobs as artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, new research shows.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How AI will shape work in 2025
item.description: AlphaSense is a market intelligence platform that uses generative artificial intelligence (genAI) and natural language processing to help organizations find and analyze insights from sources like financial reports, news, earnings calls, and proprietary documents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use the new Lock object in C# 13
item.description: The C# programming language has provided support for thread synchronization using the lock keyword since its earliest versions. By using a lock statement, you ensure that only one thread can execute the body of the statement at a time. Any other thread is blocked until the lock is released.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AWS to invest $11 billion in Georgia to expand infra for gen AI
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is planning to invest $11 billion in the state of Georgia to expand its infrastructure in the US to support the growing demand for generative AI, AI, and other cloud technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Closing the digital gap: The importance of secure development practices in government IT modernization
item.description: In today's fast-paced digital landscape, effective government IT modernization is crucial for agencies to achieve their missions. The post Closing the digital gap: The importance of secure development practices in government IT modernization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Website certificates that expire every six weeks? What IT should know
item.description: Industry forces — led by Apple and Google — are pushing for a sharp acceleration of how often website certificates must be updated, but the stated security reason is raising an awful lot of eyebrows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Almost nothing remains of Software AG
item.description: Software AG (now legally known as Software GmbH) has sold its Alfabet and Cumulocity divisions, following on from the recent sales of webMethods, StreamSets and TrendMiner.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities
item.description: The White House is prepping a new cybersecurity executive order that would address cloud security, give CISA new authorities to do threat hunting and more. The post White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Python wins Tiobe language of the year honors
item.description: To the surprise of probably no one, Python has won the Tiobe programming language of the year award for 2024, given to the language with the highest increase in ratings in Tiobe’s index of language popularity during the year.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft will invest $80B in AI data centers in fiscal 2025
item.description: Microsoft is on track to invest around $80 billion this fiscal year to build out AI-enabled data centers, company president Brad Smith said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hacked Chrome extensions put 2.6 million users at risk of data leak
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are embedding dangerous code in Chrome extensions to steal your data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
